paddle_support.low: requires efficient paddling and accurate positioning
paddle_support.high: helps entry, wave count and recovery between waves

wave_entry.demanding: needs precise timing and committed positioning
wave_entry.easy: gets moving early and enters waves with less effort

stability.high: stable platform for learning, trim and confidence

forgiveness.low: punishes poor stance, timing or rail pressure
forgiveness.high: supports imperfect timing and weight distribution

glide.low: relies more on wave power and active speed generation
glide.high: maintains momentum and crosses flat sections easily

speed_generation.low: needs wave power or active surfing
speed_generation.high: creates speed readily in weak or average waves

hold.low: loose feel with less security in power
hold.high: confidence through steep or powerful sections

release.high: easy to break free or redirect

selection_principles: Beginners benefit from paddle support, stability, forgiveness and glide before sensitivity or tight turning.
Advanced surfers may deliberately trade forgiveness for precision, hold, projection or release.
No single trait is universally best; the correct balance depends on surfer stage, waves and intent.
